characters.name: Captain Carter
characters.description: A mysterious man who spent time with the narrator's family and later died under strange circumstances, leaving behind a manuscript about his experiences.

physical.appearance: tall, dark, smooth-faced, athletic man standing over six feet, broad shouldered and narrow hipped, with regular features, black closely cropped hair, and steel gray eyes

characters.name: Edgar Rice Burroughs
characters.description: The narrator who knew Captain Carter as a child and inherited his estate and manuscript.

physical.age: child of five when first meeting Carter

characters.name: Dejah Thoris
characters.description: A character mentioned in the chapter titles and illustrations, apparently significant to Carter's story.

characters.name: Tars Tarkas
characters.description: A character mentioned in the chapter titles, apparently part of Carter's adventures.

timeline.event: Captain Carter spends months at narrator's father's home in Virginia before the Civil War
timeline.date_in_story: just prior to the Civil War opening

timeline.event: Captain Carter leaves when the war breaks out
Captain Carter returns after 15-16 years, apparently unchanged
Captain Carter moves to New York and purchases cottage on the Hudson
Narrator observes Carter writing manuscript during winter visit
Captain Carter found dead by watchman
Narrator takes charge of Carter's body and estate

settings.name: Virginia family home
settings.description: The narrator's father's home in Virginia where Captain Carter stayed before the Civil War

settings.name: Arizona
settings.description: Where Captain Carter spent time prospecting and mining after the war

settings.name: Hudson River cottage
settings.description: A small but beautiful cottage on a bluff overlooking the Hudson River in New York, where Captain Carter lived and wrote his manuscript

settings.name: Captain Carter's tomb
settings.description: A well-ventilated tomb with a massive door equipped with a gold-plated spring lock that can only be opened from the inside

world_rules: The manuscript must remain sealed and unread for eleven years after Carter's death
The manuscript's contents cannot be divulged until twenty-one years after Carter's death
Carter's tomb door can only be opened from the inside

voice_profile.style_notes: Frame narrative structure, formal Victorian prose style, building mystery and intrigue around the central character

plot_threads.thread: What is the content of Captain Carter's mysterious manuscript?
Why did Captain Carter appear not to age during his absence?
What was Carter doing during his mysterious years in Arizona?
Why was Carter's tomb designed to only open from the inside?
What was Carter reaching toward in the sky during his nighttime vigils?
